# Iteration 032: MIGRATION RISK MATRIX

## Focus
MIGRATION RISK MATRIX: for each adopt-now pattern, identify what can break during migration, plus rollback plans and feature flags.

## Findings
### Finding 1: `memory_review` is low schema risk, high workflow risk
- **Source**: [vault.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/mcp/vault.go#L885), [facts.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/vault/facts.go#L160), [fsrs-scheduler.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts#L39), [fsrs-scheduler.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts#L197), [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L529), [tool-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L297) [SOURCE: Modus explicit reinforce; Public graded FSRS primitives plus current `memory_validate` only]
- **What it does**: Modus exposes explicit reinforce-after-recall; Public already has graded FSRS review math, `nextReviewDate`, and opt-in strengthening-on-access, but no first-class graded operator tool.
- **Why it matters**: this is still the best adopt-now transfer, but the breakage will come from review-event semantics, not from missing storage.
- **Could break**: dashboards or automations that currently treat `memory_validate(wasUseful)` as the only human-feedback write path; promotion/confidence flows if teams start using `memory_review` instead of `memory_validate`; accidental write-on-read if implementation piggybacks on `trackAccess`; timestamp semantics if `last_review` starts meaning “graded human review” in some paths and “search access” in others.
- **Feature flags needed**: `SPECKIT_MEMORY_REVIEW_ENABLED` to expose the tool; `SPECKIT_MEMORY_REVIEW_SEPARATE_FROM_VALIDATE` to keep `memory_validate` unchanged while the new path stabilizes.
- **Rollback plan**: make the tool additive only; if behavior or telemetry regresses, disable the tool flag and route operators back to `memory_validate` plus existing `trackAccess=false` searches. Existing FSRS columns can stay because read paths already tolerate them.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: doctor/debug visibility can improve trust fast, but summary compression is the migration hazard
- **Source**: [doctor.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go#L13), [doctor.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go#L42), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L676), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L691), [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L382), [tool-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L236) [SOURCE: Modus one-screen doctor versus Public log/trace-heavy routing and richer repair surface]
- **What it does**: Modus turns health into a compact operator report. Public already has richer health and routing data, but much of it lives in stderr logs, trace envelopes, or full `memory_health` payloads.
- **Why it matters**: this is a clean adopt-now pattern if treated as a formatter/overlay, not as a replacement control plane.
- **Could break**: operator understanding if a compact summary hides confirmation-gated repair semantics; scripts that parse today’s `memory_health` payload if the existing shape is changed instead of extended; debugging workflows if route summaries move from logs into response payloads without clear precedence.
- **Feature flags needed**: `SPECKIT_MEMORY_HEALTH_DOCTOR_MODE` for compact health output; `SPECKIT_MEMORY_ROUTE_SUMMARY_ENABLED` for inline route/debug summaries on retrieval surfaces.
- **Rollback plan**: keep `memory_health(reportMode:"full")` and current trace payloads untouched; ship doctor/debug as opt-in profile/report modes only. If confusion appears, disable the new modes and retain the underlying health/trace machinery unchanged.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: the canonical-pipeline rule is the safest adopt-now pattern, but wrappers can still drift if they bypass `executePipeline`
- **Source**: [indexer.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/index/indexer.go#L287), [facts.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/vault/facts.go#L290), [index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/index.ts#L4), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L771), [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L10), [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L1210) [SOURCE: Modus has multiple fact-search lanes; Public already has a single runtime pipeline with explicit double-count guards]
- **What it does**: Modus mixes index-level fact search and vault-level fallback search. Public has already concentrated runtime retrieval behind `executePipeline`, with Stage 2 guarding score application and double-counting.
- **Why it matters**: the migration lesson is not “add a feature,” it is “forbid new retrieval branches.” That matters most before `memory_due`, connected-doc appendix, or lexical fallback work begins.
- **Could break**: ranking consistency if new surfaces query SQLite/FTS directly and then post-process outside the pipeline; score inflation if intent or graph adjustments are applied both inside and outside Stage 2; eval comparability if new operator tools return results shaped by sidecar ranking logic rather than canonical pipeline metadata.
- **Feature flags needed**: `SPECKIT_CANONICAL_PIPELINE_ENFORCED` to require wrapper tools to call pipeline-backed helpers only; `SPECKIT_EXPERIMENTAL_RETRIEVAL_WRAPPERS` for any trial surface that still needs isolation.
- **Rollback plan**: if any new surface drifts, disable the wrapper flag and route requests back through existing `memory_search`/`memory_context` code paths only. This rollback is cheap because the rule is architectural and mostly additive.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

## Sources Consulted
- [memory.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/mcp/memory.go#L7)
- [vault.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/mcp/vault.go#L75)
- [doctor.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go#L13)
- [facts.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/vault/facts.go#L160)
- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L771)
- [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L382)
- [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L10)
- [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md#L90044)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.16
- Questions addressed: what can break if we add `memory_review`; what can break if we expose doctor/debug summaries; what can break if we enforce wrapper-on-pipeline discipline.
- Questions answered: `memory_review` is storage-light but semantics-heavy; doctor/debug should be an overlay, not a replacement; the canonical-pipeline rule is cheap to adopt and mainly protects against future divergence.
- Novelty justification: earlier iterations ranked the adopt-now items; this pass adds migration-specific failure modes, explicit rollback boundaries, and concrete flag gating for each one.

## Ruled Out
- Shipping `memory_due` in the same tranche, because its contract risk is still higher than the three adopt-now patterns.
- Letting doctor/debug replace `memory_health(full)`, because that would hide repair and degradation semantics.
- Implementing any new operator surface with bespoke ranking, because that recreates the multi-lane drift Modus already shows.

## Reflection
- What worked: re-reading the live Public handler and pipeline code after the synthesis report, because it separated “safe additive surface” risks from “architectural drift” risks.
- What did not work: broad grep across all iteration artifacts, because the accumulated research log is noisy and duplicates late-stage conclusions.
- What I would do differently: in a write-enabled follow-up, convert these proposed flags directly into an implementation ADR so the rollout contract is frozen before code lands.

## Recommended Next Focus
Translate this matrix into implementation design in this order: `memory_review` API contract, doctor/debug response shape, then an ADR that codifies wrapper-only access to the canonical retrieval pipeline.

Note: the recent `memory_review` activity cue came from the local memory registry and may be stale; the migration analysis above is grounded in current source reads.
